title: What to know about Verbascum thapsus before using it medicinally
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Verbascum thapsus are treating cough due to its expectorant properties, which are attributed to the presence of triterpenoid saponins and flavonoids. The plant is also used to reduce fever, thanks to its ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, a property that is associated with the presence of sesquiterpenoid lactones. Additionally, Verbascum thapsus is used to alleviate diarrhea, a condition that is linked to the anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects of its flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties, which are mediated by the sesquiterpenoid lactones, also make it useful for treating inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as managing respiratory issues such as bronchitis, which is associated with the presence of flavonoids and saponins. Furthermore, Verbascum thapsus is used to treat conditions like eczema, asthma, and rheumatism, which are all linked to its anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, particularly those mediated by its flavonoids and sesquiterpenoid lactones.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Verbascum thapsus are attributed to its antimicrobial activity, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and saponins. It also exhibits antioxidant properties, particularly due to the presence of phenolic acids, which play a crucial role in protecting cells from oxidative damage and potentially inhibiting the growth of various microorganisms. Verbascum thapsus is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which can be linked to its content of volatile oils, such as isovalerianic acid, that can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain. Additionally, the plant has analgesic and antispasmodic properties, which are thought to be caused by the presence of iridoids and triterpenoids that can help relieve pain and muscle spasms. The sedative, anticough, and expectorant properties of Verbascum thapsus may be attributed to its content of sesquiterpene lactones, which can help induce relaxation and relieve respiratory issues.
identification: Verbascum thapsus is a biennial plant belonging to the Scrophulariaceae family, characterized by its large, coarse, hairy leaves, and tall, upright stem that can reach heights of up to 2 meters. Its leaves are ovate, 15-30 cm long, and 5-10 cm wide, with a wavy or toothed margin. The plant's geographical distribution is widespread across Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, and it has also been naturalized in other parts of the world, including North America and Australia. It thrives in a variety of habitats, including roadsides, waste areas, and disturbed ground, and can tolerate a range of soil types and moisture levels. The plant produces yellow flowers in clusters, 2-5 cm in diameter, with five petals and a prominent cluster of stamens in the center.
cultivation: Verbascum thapsus is a biennial plant that grows up to 1.5 meters tall, with a spread of 0.5 meters, and exhibits a rosette habit in its first year, followed by a tall, erect stem in the second year, producing a large number of small, yellow flowers. It thrives in full sun to partial shade, and well-drained soil with a pH between 6.0 and 7.0, tolerating a range of soil types but preferring fertile, sandy, or chalky ground. To cultivate Verbascum thapsus successfully, it is essential to provide it with adequate moisture, especially during its first year, but avoid waterlogging, and fertilize lightly in the second year to promote flowering. Sowing seeds directly into the ground in the spring or autumn, and thinning them to 30-40 cm apart, will help to reduce competition and promote healthy growth. Regular deadheading can also encourage the plant to produce more flowers and prevent self-seeding, which can lead to the spread of the plant in unwanted areas.
history: Verbascum thapsus is a plant that has been used in various forms of traditional medicine for centuries, including in ancient Greece and Rome, where it was employed to treat a range of conditions including bronchitis, asthma, and sore throats due to its mucilaginous properties. The plant's leaves and roots were used in folk medicine to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, and to reduce inflammation. In the 18th and 19th centuries, Verbascum thapsus was used to treat respiratory issues including coughs and colds, with the leaves being made into a tea or infusion. The plant's antiviral properties were also recognized, with some studies suggesting its use in treating herpes simplex and other viral infections. The medicinal properties of Verbascum thapsus have been attributed to the presence of compounds such as verbascoside and isoverbascoside, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities.

constituents.name: Flavonoids
constituents.description: Verbascum thapsus flavonoids are antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial compounds

constituents.name: Saponins
constituents.description: Verbascum thapsus saponins are bitter, foaming compounds with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic acids
constituents.description: Phenolic acids are hydroxylated, benzene-ring-based compounds, which exhibit antioxidant and free radical scavenging properties, as well as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities.

constituents.name: Coumarins
constituents.description: Coumarins are a class of medicinal constituents found in Verbascum thapsus that exhibit antiseptic, antispasmodic, and diuretic properties.

constituents.name: Alkaloids
constituents.description: Verbascum thapsus contains Verbascoside and other Alkaloids which are known to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Triterpenoids
constituents.description: Verbascum thapsus contains triterpenoids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Glycosides
constituents.description: Glycosides in Verbascum thapsus have been found to have expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Terpenoids
constituents.description: Verbascum thapsus contains terpenoids, which have antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Sterols
constituents.description: Sterols, found in Verbascum thapsus, possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Iridoids
constituents.description: Verbascum thapsus iridoids have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial and antiviral properties.

constituents.name: Lignans
constituents.description: Verbascum thapsus contains lignans, which possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Tannins
constituents.description: Tannins are astringent compounds in Verbascum thapsus that exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic glycosides
constituents.description: Phenolic glycosides are phenolic compounds that have a sugar molecule attached, exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial activities

constituents.name: Triterpenes
constituents.description: Triterpenes are constituents of Verbascum thapsus with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties

constituents.name: Cardiac glycosides
constituents.description: Verbascum thapsus contains cardiac glycosides with inotropic and chronotropic properties.

constituents.name: Phytosterols
constituents.description: Phytosterols are a type of plant sterol that are found in Verbascum thapsus, having anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic Acids
constituents.description: Phenolic Acids are a type of bioactive compound found in Verbascum thapsus, characterized by their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Steroids
constituents.description: Verbascoside is a steroid glycoside with anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic properties.

constituents.name: Fatty acids
constituents.description: Verbascum thapsus contains fatty acids, which are known for their hydrophobic, oily properties and have been identified to consist of various saturated and unsaturated chains.

constituents.name: Verbascoside
constituents.description: Verbascoside is a triterpenoid saponin with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant and antimicrobial properties.

side_effects.side_effect_list_description: Verbascum thapsus contains saponins that stimulate gastric contractions and induce vomiting.
Ingestion of Verbascum thapsus causes gastrointestinal irritation leading to diarrhea.
Verbascum thapsus affects uterine contractions by causing them to become stronger and more frequent.
Verbascum thapsus contains thapsigenin, a compound that has been shown to induce abortion in animals
Verbascum thapsus contains scilliroside, which can induce seizures in sensitive individuals, particularly in high doses.
Verbascum thapsus contains thapsigargin, a compound that inhibits calcium ATPase, leading to respiratory depression.
Verbascum thapsus contains cardenolides that can act as cardiac glycosides, leading to cardiac arrhythmias.
Verbascum thapsus ingestion causes tremors due to its alkaloid content.
Verbascum thapsus contains saponins and flavonoids that induce excessive heat production in the body.
Verbascum thapsus contains toxic glycosides that cause renal failure if ingested in large amounts.

intro_description: Verbascum thapsus, commonly known as great mullein, is a medicinal herb used to treat various health conditions. Its main medicinal uses include treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and allergies. The herb has main health benefits of anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Its therapeutic properties include expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. Bioactive compounds found in Verbascum thapsus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins. Herbal preparations include infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, often used to treat skin issues and wounds.

uses_list.description: This plant is used for asthma because it contains a number of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and iridoid glycosides, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to alleviate the symptoms of asthma.
Its use for fever is attributed to its antipyretic properties, which are thought to be due to the presence of flavonoids that can help to lower body temperature and reduce the severity of fever.

The plant is used for diarrhea because it contains mucilages, which are complex carbohydrates that can help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the gastrointestinal tract, reducing inflammation and promoting the healing of damaged tissues.

It is used for coughs because its mucilages can help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and promoting the healing of damaged tissues, which can help to alleviate the symptoms of coughs.

The plant is used for inflammation because it contains a number of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and iridoid glycosides, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to alleviate the symptoms of inflammation.

Its use for bronchitis is attributed to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to cough up, and reduce the inflammation and discomfort associated with bronchitis.

The plant is used for eczema because its mucilages can help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and promoting the healing of damaged tissues, which can help to alleviate the symptoms of eczema.
Its use for rheumatism is attributed to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help to reduce the pain and inflammation associated with rheumatism.

The plant is used for sore throats because its mucilages can help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and promoting the healing of damaged tissues, which can help to alleviate the symptoms of sore throats.

Its use for pneumonia is attributed to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to cough up, and reduce the inflammation and discomfort associated with pneumonia.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Verbascum thapsus is used to treat various health conditions. Its antitussive and anti-inflammatory properties make it effective in alleviating cough and inflammation associated with bronchitis. The plant's demulcent and soothing properties help to calm irritated skin and mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms of eczema and skin irritation. Verbascum thapsus is also used to treat respiratory issues such as asthma and sinusitis, as well as gastrointestinal problems like diarrhea and gastroenteritis, fever, pneumonia, influenza, tonsillitis, sore throat, dyspepsia, and allergies, which may be triggered by mucus buildup and coughs. Additionally, its antiviral properties may help to combat viral infections like warts.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional Chinese medicine, Verbascum thapsus is used to treat coughs, particularly those accompanied by colds and congestion. It has also been employed to alleviate diarrhea and fever, often in cases where the body is weakened by an underlying illness. Additionally, the plant is used to combat bronchitis, as well as inflammation and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory properties. Furthermore, Verbascum thapsus has been used to treat rheumatism, pneumonia, gastroenteritis, sore throats, malaria, dysentery, influenza, dyspepsia, tuberculosis, colic, skin irritation, respiratory infection, skin infections, and menstrual pain, often in combination with other herbal remedies. The plant's antispasmodic properties also make it useful for calming the nervous system and relieving respiratory and gastrointestinal distress.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Verbascum thapsus is used to treat a variety of conditions, including asthma, where its expectorant properties help relieve respiratory issues, fever, which is reduced by its antipyretic properties, and diarrhea, due to its ability to regulate bowel movements. Additionally, it is used to treat inflammation, such as rheumatism, which is alleviated by its anti-inflammatory properties. Furthermore, its antimicrobial and antifungal properties make it effective in treating conditions like eczema and bronchitis, while its expectorant properties also help relieve cough and sore throat. Moreover, it is used to treat tuberculosis, dyspepsia, and diabetes, which are managed by its antioxidant and anti-diabetic properties. Also, its antimicrobial properties make it effective in treating conditions like influenza, wounds, gastroenteritis, skin infection, burns, malaria, and pneumonia, as well as hypertension, which is regulated by its vasodilatory properties.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Verbascum thapsus is used to treat asthma by its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is also employed to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of diarrhea. Additionally, it is utilized to cure cough, rheumatism, and eczema, and has been found to be effective in treating tonsillitis by reducing inflammation. Furthermore, Verbascum thapsus is used to combat influenza, skin ulcers, and skin infections, as well as gastritis and dyspepsia. The plant's medicinal properties have also been found to be beneficial in treating bronchitis, pneumonia, gastroenteritis, sore throat, diabetes, tuberculosis, and hypertension, making it a valuable remedy in unani medicine.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Verbascum thapsus is used to treat inflammation, asthma, and bronchitis, often with the goal of reducing respiratory distress and promoting recovery. Additionally, it is used to alleviate fever, diarrhea, and eczema, as well as to address sore throat and cough symptoms, which are frequently associated with respiratory issues. Verbascum thapsus is also employed to treat rheumatism, pneumonia, and coughs, with the aim of reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the affected areas. Furthermore, it is used to address tuberculosis, skin irritation, fungal infections, warts, gastritis, allergies, and nausea, which can be symptoms of a variety of underlying conditions. By addressing these conditions, Verbascum thapsus is also seen as a treatment for ear infections, respiratory issues, and other related health problems.

benefits_list.description: This plant contains bioactive compounds that exhibit antimicrobial properties, which allow it to inhibit the growth of a wide range of microorganisms, including bacteria, fungi, and yeast, thereby providing protection against infections and promoting wound healing.

The plant contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which act as potent antioxidants, neutralizing free radicals and preventing oxidative stress, thus protecting cells from damage and reducing the risk of chronic diseases.

The bioactive compounds present in the plant have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with various inflammatory conditions, such as arthritis, by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.

The plant's bioactive compounds have been shown to exhibit antiviral activity, which enables them to inhibit the replication of certain viruses, thereby reducing the severity of viral infections and promoting the recovery process.

The plant's bioactive compounds have antibacterial properties, which allow them to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria, thereby reducing the risk of bacterial infections and promoting wound healing.
The plant's bioactive compounds have antifungal properties, which enable them to inhibit the growth of certain fungi, thereby reducing the risk of fungal infections and promoting wound healing.

The bioactive compounds present in the plant have cardioprotective effects, which help to reduce the risk of cardiovascular diseases by lowering blood pressure, improving lipid profiles, and preventing the formation of blood clots.

The plant's bioactive compounds have neuroprotective properties, which enable them to reduce the risk of neurodegenerative diseases, such as Alzheimer's and Parkinson's, by protecting neurons from oxidative stress and inflammation, and promoting neuronal health.

The plant's bioactive compounds have anticancer properties, which enable them to inhibit the growth of cancer cells, induce apoptosis, and prevent the formation of new blood vessels that supply the tumor, thereby reducing the risk of cancer development and progression.

The plant's bioactive compounds have immunomodulatory effects, which enable them to stimulate the immune system, increase the production of antibodies, and enhance the activity of immune cells, thereby reducing the risk of infections and promoting overall health.

benefits_description: Verbascum thapsus antimicrobial action prevents the growth of various pathogens, including bacteria, fungi, and viruses. The plant's antioxidant properties protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can lead to cell death and disease. Verbascum thapsus also exhibits antiinflammatory effects, reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of conditions such as arthritis. Additionally, Verbascum thapsus has antiviral activity, which inhibits the replication of viruses and prevents the spread of viral infections. Furthermore, the plant has antibacterial action, antifungal properties, cardioprotective effects, neuroprotective action, anticancer properties, and immune system stimulation, all of which contribute to its medicinal value.
benefits_circulatory_system_description: Verbascum thapsus improves blood flow through its flavonoid content, which has vasodilatory properties, allowing for increased blood flow and oxygen delivery to tissues. Reduced blood pressure is also facilitated by its diuretic properties, which help regulate fluid balance and electrolyte levels in the body, thereby lowering blood pressure. This reduction in blood pressure allows for increased oxygenation of the body's tissues, as the cardiovascular system is able to pump blood more efficiently. By enhancing circulation, Verbascum thapsus helps to lower cholesterol levels, as the increased blood flow and oxygen delivery to the liver enable more efficient removal of excess cholesterol. Additionally, its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties prevent thrombosis, stabilize vasculature, boost cardiac output, regulate hemodynamics, and reduce inflammation, thereby maintaining a healthy and balanced circulatory system.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Verbascum thapsus alleviates digestive issues by reducing inflammation in the gut, which in turn prevents diarrhea due to its antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of pathogenic microorganisms. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties also soothe irritation in the digestive tract and relieve bloating by reducing the production of inflammatory mediators. Furthermore, Verbascum thapsus has antioxidant properties that heal gastritis by neutralizing free radicals and preventing oxidative damage to the gastric mucosa, while its regulatory effects on bowel movements cure constipation by stimulating the motility of the gastrointestinal tract. Additionally, the plant's ability to treat ulcers is attributed to its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which inhibit the growth of Helicobacter pylori and promote the healing of ulcers. Verbascum thapsus eases abdominal pain by regulating bowel movements and reducing inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, thus providing relief from gastritis and other digestive disorders.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Verbascum thapsus regulates thyroid by modulating the production of thyroid hormones, primarily through its flavonoid and phenolic compounds, which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. This plant stabilizes blood sugar by enhancing insulin sensitivity due to its glycosidic and phenolic compounds, which modulate glucose metabolism and improve glucose uptake in cells. Verbascum thapsus balances adrenal hormones by modulating the activity of enzymes involved in steroidogenesis, thereby reducing cortisol levels, which are regulated by its flavonoid and phenolic compounds with antioxidant properties. The plant improves insulin sensitivity and enhances thyroid function by increasing the production of thyroid hormones, primarily through its flavonoid and phenolic compounds, which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Verbascum thapsus also supports gonadal health, regenerates pancreatic tissue, and normalizes ovarian function by modulating the production of sex hormones and enhancing the function of the pancreas and ovaries due to its glycosidic, phenolic, and flavonoid compounds with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Verbascum thapsus soothes skin irritation due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of pathogenic microorganisms and reduce redness and swelling. It protects skin barrier by forming a hydrophobic layer on the skin surface, preventing water loss and enhancing skin hydration. The plant's antioxidant properties, particularly its flavonoids and phenolic acids, reduce inflammation quickly and promote healing by scavenging free radicals and enhancing collagen synthesis. Verbascum thapsus fights skin infections by exerting antimicrobial and antifungal activities, inhibiting the growth of microorganisms and preventing infection. It also saturates skin moisture by enhancing skin hydration, heals wound faster by promoting tissue regeneration, calms skin redness by reducing inflammation, stimulates skin renewal by increasing cell turnover, restores skin elasticity by enhancing collagen synthesis, and prevents skin aging by protecting against UV-induced damage and oxidative stress.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Verbascum thapsus boosts lymph flow by containing saponins, which stimulate lymphatic vessels, enhancing the circulation of lymph fluid throughout the body. This plant reduces swelling faster due to its anti-inflammatory properties, specifically its sesquiterpene lactones, which inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. The antimicrobial and antioxidant properties of Verbascum thapsus improve the detoxification process by eliminating toxins and free radicals, while its immunomodulatory effects enhance the immune response. The plant increases white blood cells by stimulating the production of cytokines, which activate immune cells, and reduces inflammation quickly due to its flavonoids, which inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory mediators. Verbascum thapsus also promotes healthy tissues, supports natural filtering, fights off infections, and stimulates lymphatic drainage, making it a valuable herb for maintaining a healthy lymphatic system.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Verbascum thapsus reduces joint inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, thereby alleviating joint pain and discomfort. This plant relieves muscle tension by containing flavonoids that exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, mitigating muscle spasms and promoting relaxation. Verbascum thapsus eases arthritis pain by modulating the immune response and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators, thus reducing joint inflammation and pain. Additionally, it fights bone disease by increasing bone density and promoting osteogenesis, making it an effective treatment for conditions such as osteoporosis and osteopenia. Verbascum thapsus cures tendonitis by reducing inflammation and promoting the regeneration of damaged tissue, thereby alleviating soreness and restoring mobility to affected joints and muscles.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Verbascum thapsus relieves stress by containing rosmarinic acid, a compound that exhibits anti-anxiety properties, thereby reducing anxiety. Calms nerves are soothed by the sedative effects of scopoletin, a lignan present in the plant, which helps to stabilize mood by modulating neurotransmitter activity. Verbascum thapsus also soothes irritability due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to the reduction of fatigue. The plant improves sleep by promoting relaxation through the inhibition of the nervous system's activity, while enhancing focus is facilitated by the flavonoids present in Verbascum thapsus, which have been shown to improve cognitive function. Additionally, Verbascum thapsus regulates hormones by exerting antioxidant effects that protect against oxidative stress, thereby boosting energy and decreasing fatigue.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Verbascum thapsus regulates ovulation by exerting anti-inflammatory properties that help maintain a healthy reproductive environment. The plant's antioxidant properties reduce infertility by protecting the reproductive system from oxidative stress and DNA damage. Preventing miscarriage is also a benefit of Verbascum thapsus, as its antimicrobial properties inhibit the growth of pathogens that can cause infections during pregnancy. Increases in fertility are linked to the plant's ability to stimulate hormone production and improve blood flow to the reproductive organs, which enhances the chances of conception. By reducing menstrual cramps and regulating the menstrual cycle, treating polycystic ovary syndrome, relieving endometriosis symptoms, enhancing folic acid levels, improving menstrual regularity, and stimulating hormone production, Verbascum thapsus provides comprehensive reproductive health benefits.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Verbascum thapsus relieve congestion by exerting antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of pathogens responsible for respiratory infections. The anti-inflammatory properties of Verbascum thapsus reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe, thus ease breathing. Furthermore, the plant's ability to open airways and prevent asthma attacks is attributed to its antioxidant and expectorant properties, which help to clear mucus and debris from the lungs. Its relaxant properties also allow for the relaxation of tissues in the respiratory tract, while its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties work synergistically to soothe the lungs and calm allergies. Additionally, the immune-boosting effects of Verbascum thapsus strengthen the immune system, enabling it to fight off infections and diseases more effectively.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Verbascum thapsus reduces inflammation through its anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Preventing infections in the urinary system is also achieved due to its antimicrobial properties, particularly the sesquiterpenoids and diterpenoids that inhibit the growth of various bacteria and fungi. Relaxing muscles in the urinary tract is facilitated by the muscle-relaxing properties of the plant's flavonoids and alkaloids, such as verbascoside. The antioxidant properties of Verbascum thapsus, including the presence of phenolic acids and flavonoids, boost immunity by neutralizing free radicals and protecting against oxidative stress, which helps to cleanse the kidneys and prevent the formation of stones by removing harmful substances and minerals. Alleviating pain and regulating pH in the urinary system are also achieved through the analgesic and alkalinizing properties of the plant's compounds, such as verbascoside, which release toxins and support the normal functioning of the kidneys.

properties_list.description: Verbascum thapsus has been found to exhibit antimicrobial properties, particularly against certain strains of bacteria such as Staphylococcus aureus and Escherichia coli, demonstrating its potential use in the development of novel antimicrobial agents.

The plant Verbascum thapsus has been reported to possess antioxidant properties, attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which can help protect cells from oxidative damage and potentially mitigate the progression of age-related diseases.

Verbascum thapsus has been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, primarily due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with various inflammatory conditions.

The plant Verbascum thapsus has been traditionally used as an antispasmodic agent to alleviate spasms and relieve symptoms associated with gastrointestinal disorders, such as irritable bowel syndrome.

Research has shown that Verbascum thapsus possesses antiviral properties, particularly against certain strains of herpes simplex virus and human immunodeficiency virus, highlighting its potential use in the development of novel antiviral agents.

Verbascum thapsus has been traditionally used as a sedative agent to promote relaxation and relieve symptoms associated with anxiety and insomnia, primarily due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic compounds.
The plant Verbascum thapsus has been reported to exhibit antihistamine properties, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with allergic reactions, such as itching and inflammation.

Verbascum thapsus has been traditionally used as an expectorant agent to relieve symptoms associated with respiratory disorders, such as bronchitis and asthma, primarily due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic compounds.

The plant Verbascum thapsus has been found to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation associated with various conditions, such as arthritis and musculoskeletal disorders.

parts_description: The most used parts of Verbascum thapsus for medicinal purposes are flowers and leaves. The flowers of Verbascum thapsus contain flavonoids, such as verbascoside and verbascoside derivatives, and phenolic compounds, which exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. These compounds contribute to the flowers' primary medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help alleviate respiratory issues and improve wound healing. The leaves of Verbascum thapsus contain saponins, primarily verbascoside and isoverbascoside, which possess anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, contributing to their potential in treating skin conditions and respiratory infections. The bioactive compounds in these plant parts demonstrate the medicinal potential of Verbascum thapsus in addressing various health concerns.

parts_list.name: flowers
parts_list.description: The flowers of this plant are rich in flavonoids, including verbascoside and verbascoside glycosides, and contain compounds like ursolic acid and oleanolic acid, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and supporting the immune system, while also reducing inflammation and fighting off infections.

parts_list.name: leaves
parts_list.description: The leaves of this plant contain bioactive compounds like iridoid glycosides, particularly aucubin, and verbascoside, which possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and are traditionally used to treat respiratory problems, such as coughs and colds, and to support the immune system, while also exhibiting antioxidant and anti-allergic effects.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tincture made from this plant is used to treat coughs, colds, fever, and respiratory issues, and has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
preparations.preparation_overview: Verbascum thapsus tincture is a herbal preparation commonly used to treat a range of health issues including bronchitis, cough, respiratory problems, inflammation, digestive issues, sinusitis, sore throat and tonsillitis. It is widely used due to its effectiveness in alleviating symptoms associated with these conditions. One common effect of Verbascum thapsus tincture is its ability to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation. This preparation is typically made by infusing roots, flowers and stems of the Verbascum thapsus plant in a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin. The process of making Verbascum thapsus tincture is moderately difficult, requiring some knowledge of herbal extraction techniques and equipment.
preparation_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried roots, flowers, and stems of Verbascum thapsus, and chop them into small pieces to increase their surface area.
Combine the chopped roots, flowers, and stems in a clean glass jar, leaving enough space for liquid to cover the plant material.
Pour a solvent, such as 80% vodka or glycerin, over the plant material to cover it completely, and seal the jar to prevent contamination.
Place the jar in a cool, dark place and let it steep for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally to facilitate the extraction process.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the plant material, and store the tincture in a cool, dark place.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Salve made from this plant is used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and minor burns due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
preparations.preparation_overview: Verbascum thapsus salve is a topical preparation used to treat various skin issues such as wounds, bruises, burns, sprains, insect bites and skin irritation. It is a common remedy used by many, and its effectiveness is well-documented. The salve has a soothing effect, which helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing. It is typically made from the roots, leaves, and stems of the Verbascum thapsus plant, which are combined and infused in a carrier oil to create the salve. The process of making Verbascum thapsus salve is moderately difficult, requiring some effort and patience to extract the active compounds from the plant material and blend them with the carrier oil.
preparation_recipe: Harvest fresh Verbascum thapsus roots, leaves, and stems, making sure to identify the plant correctly to avoid any confusion or misidentification.
Dry the collected roots, leaves, and stems separately in a warm, dry, dark place for at least two weeks, until they become brittle and crumbly.
Grind the dried roots, leaves, and stems into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, ensuring to break them down into a uniform consistency.
Combine the powdered roots, leaves, and stems in a heat-resistant mixing bowl, and add a carrier oil such as olive or coconut oil to create a mixture.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof glass bowl set over a pot of simmering water, stirring occasionally, until the mixture thickens and turns into a smooth, syrupy salve.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Decoction made from this plant is used to treat respiratory issues, coughs, and bronchitis due to its expectorant properties, and also to soothe sore throats and reduce inflammation.
preparations.preparation_overview: Verbascum thapsus decoction is used to treat cough, diarrhea, fever, inflammation, skin issues, and respiratory issues. It is commonly used in herbal medicine due to its widespread availability and effectiveness. This preparation has a common effect of reducing inflammation and soothing irritated tissues. Verbascum thapsus decoction is made from the roots, leaves, and stems of the Verbascum thapsus plant, which are combined in varying proportions to create the decoction. The process of making the decoction is moderately difficult, requiring careful measurement and combination of the plant parts, followed by simmering in water to release the active compounds.
preparation_recipe: Gather a sufficient quantity of fresh Verbascum thapsus roots, leaves, and stems.
Wash the collected roots, leaves, and stems thoroughly with clean water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine the cleaned roots, leaves, and stems in a large pot and cover them with water.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer and let it steep for an extended period.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding the solids.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Ointment made from this plant is used to treat skin irritations, wounds, and minor burns, as well as to soothe coughs and respiratory issues.
preparations.preparation_overview: Verbascum thapsus ointment is a herbal preparation used to treat wounds, burns, eczema, skin irritations, boils, insect bites, bruises, sprains and fungal infections. It is a common remedy in traditional medicine due to its widespread use. The ointment has anti-inflammatory properties, which is its most common effect. Verbascum thapsus ointment is made from roots, leaves and stems of the Verbascum thapsus plant. However, its preparation requires a significant amount of plant material and is relatively complex and time-consuming to make.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh or dried roots, leaves, and stems of Verbascum thapsus plant.
Dry the gathered plant material in a cool, dark place to preserve its properties.
Combine the dried roots, leaves, and stems in a mortar and grind them into a fine powder.
Mix the powdered plant material with a carrier oil such as olive or coconut oil in a heat-resistant container.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the oil has infused with the plant properties.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Poultice made from this plant is used to treat wounds, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of eczema, boils, and ulcers.
preparations.preparation_overview: Verbascum thapsus poultice is a traditional herbal remedy commonly used to treat a variety of conditions, including boils, gangrene, abscesses, frostbite, bruises, sprains, and insect bites. This preparation is widely used due to its effectiveness and accessibility. One of the common effects of Verbascum thapsus poultice is its ability to draw out infection and promote healing. The poultice is typically made by infusing the leaves and stems of the Verbascum thapsus plant in water, which requires some effort and knowledge of herbalism, making it moderately difficult to prepare.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh Verbascum thapsus leaves and stems from a trusted source.
Rinse the leaves and stems with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the leaves and stems into small pieces to release their medicinal properties.
Soak the chopped leaves and stems in hot water for several minutes to make them pliable.
Squeeze out excess water and apply the resulting herb paste as a poultice to the affected area.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tea made from this plant is traditionally used to treat coughs, bronchitis, and sore throats due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties.
preparations.preparation_overview: Verbascum thapsus tea is commonly used to treat bronchitis, respiratory issues, cough, inflammation, sore throat and urinary tract infections. It's also used to aid wound healing. This preparation is widely used worldwide, making it one of the most popular herbal remedies. One of its most notable effects is its ability to reduce inflammation and soothe respiratory passages. Verbascum thapsus tea is typically made by infusing leaves, stems and flowers in hot water. The preparation process is straightforward and requires minimal effort, as a few minutes of steeping is all that's needed to release its medicinal properties.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh leaves, stems, and flowers of Verbascum thapsus plant.
Wash the collected plant parts thoroughly with clean water to remove dirt and impurities.
Combine equal parts of leaves, stems, and flowers in a heat-resistant container or tea infuser.
Steep the plant mixture in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbs to release their properties.
Strain the tea mixture and discard the solids to serve the Verbascum thapsus herbal tea.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Suppository made from this plant is used to treat cough, bronchitis, and inflammation of the mucous membranes, and to reduce fever and relieve sore throats.
preparations.preparation_overview: Verbascum thapsus suppository is a herbal preparation used to treat various conditions, including prostatitis, hemorrhoids, incontinence, prolapse and urinary tract infections. It is an uncommonly used treatment, likely due to its relatively unknown effects and limited research. One of the known effects of Verbascum thapsus suppository is its ability to reduce inflammation and relieve pain, but its efficacy can vary widely from person to person. The suppository is typically made from the roots and stems of the Verbascum thapsus plant, which requires specialized knowledge and equipment to process into a usable form. Due to these factors, creating a Verbascum thapsus suppository is a challenging and time-consuming process, making it a less accessible treatment option.
preparation_recipe: Gather and clean the roots of Verbascum thapsus plant and dry them in a cool and dark place.
Crush the dried roots and stems of Verbascum thapsus plant into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered plant material with a binding agent such as beeswax or coconut oil to form a paste.
Shape the paste into the desired suppository form using a mold or spoon.
Allow the suppositories to harden and then store them in an airtight container for later use.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Verbascum thapsus are induce vomiting, cause diarrhea, and affect uterine contractions due to the presence of toxic compounds, which can lead to induce abortion in pregnant individuals. Additionally, these compounds may trigger seizures in some individuals, a potentially life-threatening side effect. Furthermore, improper use of Verbascum thapsus can cause respiratory depression, leading to inadequate oxygen intake, as well as induce cardiac arrhythmias, disrupting normal heart rhythms. Other side effects include trigger tremors, a symptom of neurological disturbance, and cause hyperthermia, a potentially fatal increase in body temperature. In severe cases, renal failure may also occur, a result of the plant's toxic effects on the kidneys.

precautions.precaution_list_description: Verbascum thapsus contains saponins, which may cause uterine stimulation and abortifacient effects, making it contraindicated during pregnancy.
Used in traditional medicine, Verbascum thapsus may cause allergic reactions or interact with medications, requiring medical guidance.
High Verbascum thapsus pollen levels may exacerbate hypertension in susceptible individuals.
Verbascum thapsus may lower blood sugar levels, causing hypoglycemia when taken with diabetes medications.
Initial treatment with Verbascum thapsus involves administering small amounts due to its potential for toxicity and narrow therapeutic index.
Verbascum thapsus sap causes skin irritation and allergic contact dermatitis.
Verbascum thapsus contains glycosides, which can cause liver or kidney damage in individuals with pre-existing liver or kidney disease.
Individuals with liver or kidney disease should consult their medical history before using Verbascum thapsus due to its potential for saponin toxicity.
Combining Verbascum thapsus with sedatives may produce enhanced sedative effects due to its barbiturate-like activity.
Verbascum thapsus sap contains saponins, which can cause gastrointestinal upset if ingested by children.

intro_study: According to a study published by Phytotherapy research: PTR, Verbascum thapsus has been found to possess various biological and pharmacological properties, including antiviral, antioxidant, and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help in mitigating various diseases. It also exhibits analgesic and sedative properties, making it a potential treatment for relieving pain and promoting relaxation. Additionally, Verbascum thapsus has been shown to have anticancer properties, highlighting its potential in cancer treatment and prevention.

According to a study published by "Medicinal chemistry (Shariqah (United Arab Emirates))", Verbascum thapsus has been found to exhibit strong antioxidant activities, with the highest inhibition of DPPH free radicals at a concentration of 300 mg/l, comparable to the antioxidant activities of synthesized antioxidant BHT. Additionally, the study revealed that Verbascum thapsus possesses antimicrobial properties, with a concentration of 50 mg/l resulting in only 21.26% inhibition of DPPH free radicals, while Bacillus cereus was found to be the most sensitive bacterial strain among the tested strains. Furthermore, the study suggests that Verbascum thapsus may be effective in the treatment of various diseases, including cancer and infectious diseases, due to its anticarcinogenic and antimicrobial activities.

According to a study published by Microscopy Research and Technique, Verbascum thapsus exhibits several beneficial properties, including the presence of various secondary metabolites in its methanolic extract, which suggests its potential pharmacological activities. The plant's unique anatomical features, such as long and stellate type trichomes, collateral type of vascular bundles, and a well-developed pith, help in the absorption and retention of water, contributing to its survival as a biennial plant. The study also reveals that Verbascum thapsus contains essential macro and microelements, which could be beneficial for both humans and animals.
properties_description: The therapeutic properties of Verbascum thapsus are attributed to its antimicrobial activity, which is mediated by phenolic compounds, allowing it to inhibit the growth of various pathogens. Its antioxidant properties, conferred by flavonoids and ascorbic acid, protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. The plant's anti-inflammatory effect, due to the presence of iridoid glycosides and sesquiterpenes, reduces inflammation and alleviates conditions such as arthritis. Verbascum thapsus also exhibits antispasmodic properties, thanks to the action of sesquiterpenes and alkaloids, which relax smooth muscles and alleviate spasms. Additionally, the plant's antiviral properties, attributed to the presence of flavonoids and triterpenes, inhibit viral replication and alleviate symptoms of viral infections, and it also shows sedative effects due to the action of volatile oils, antihistamine activity due to the presence of flavonoids, expectorant properties due to the action of glycosides, and pain-relieving properties due to the presence of sesquiterpenes and alkaloids.
constituents_description: The healing constituents of Verbascum thapsus are flavonoids, saponins, phenolic acids, coumarins, and alkaloids. Flavonoids such as kaempferol, quercetin, and isorhapontigenin present in Verbascum thapsus have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Saponins have been reported to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. Phenolic acids including ferulic and sinapic acid possess antioxidant and free radical scavenging properties. Coumarins and alkaloids present in Verbascum thapsus have been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antitumor activities.
preparation_description: The most common herbal preparation of Verbascum thapsus for medicinal purposes are tincture, which is used topically for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties to treat minor wounds and skin irritations. The salve, often used for skin conditions like eczema and acne, provides a soothing and protective barrier. Decoction is sometimes employed to create an expectorant for relieving coughs and respiratory issues, while ointment is used to treat skin conditions like ringworm and fungal infections. A poultice is applied to relieve pain and inflammation in specific areas, such as sprains and strains. Tea made from the plant is consumed to treat respiratory issues like bronchitis and coughs, and suppositories are used to treat gastrointestinal issues like diarrhea and constipation.
precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Verbascum thapsus medicinally are contraindicating its use in pregnancy due to potential harm to the fetus, and it is recommended to use it under medical supervision to monitor its efficacy and potential side effects. Furthermore, patients with hypertension should be closely monitored for changes in blood pressure, and those with diabetes should avoid interactions with Verbascum thapsus as it may potentiate the effects of diabetes medications. Additionally, the use of Verbascum thapsus should be initiated with low doses to assess tolerance and minimize side effects, and patients should be watched for signs of allergic reactions such as skin rashes or itching. Those with pre-existing liver or kidney disease should avoid using Verbascum thapsus as it may exacerbate their condition, and its use should be carefully considered in individuals with a medical history of sensitivity to plants. Finally, Verbascum thapsus should not be used with sedatives as it may enhance their sedative effects, and it is not recommended for use in children due to lack of established safety and efficacy.
